registry_digest_binding: AAOS-RFC-0000-C0.component_digests and the Phase 5 external verification receipt

requirements.title: Stable RFC identity
requirements.normative_statement: Every AAOS RFC MUST have one stable RFC identifier that remains distinct from its version, repository path, issue, pull request, commit, tree, rendered document digest, and release identifier.
required_inputs: predecessor and successor references when applicable
required_evidence: predecessor and successor references where applicable
requirements.positive_case: The RFC ID is stable and separately records version, repository, issue, pull request, Git identities, document digest, and release identity.
requirements.negative_case: An RFC ID is reused or treated as interchangeable with a version, mutable path, issue, pull request, Git identity, digest, or release identifier.
requirements.adversarial_case: A mutable branch or issue title is presented as the stable RFC identity.
requirements.unknown_case: Identifier uniqueness or predecessor history cannot be established from the supplied registry.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Return not_evaluable and block identity-dependent review or disposition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: ambiguous or reused RFC identity -> blocked_by_contract_contradiction
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Stable identity evidence does not assign lifecycle status, implementation state, conformance, deployment, or institutional adoption.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; no private role, trusted root, threshold, or institutional Authority is asserted

requirements.title: Exact candidate binding
requirements.normative_statement:
Every material review disposition and every RFC lifecycle transition MUST bind the exact candidate version and canonical serialized candidate digest.

When materialized in a repository, the disposition MUST also bind repository, commit, and tree identity.
required_evidence: repository, commit, and tree identity when materialized
requirements.positive_case: A disposition identifies the exact candidate version and canonical digest and, after materialization, the exact repository, commit, and tree.
requirements.negative_case: issue number, title, branch, or mutable path only -> insufficient candidate identity
requirements.adversarial_case: A reviewer substitutes a later branch head or same-named file for the disposition-bound candidate.
requirements.unknown_case: Any mandatory digest or materialized repository identity is absent or unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Return not_evaluable and prohibit the material disposition or lifecycle transition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: The review may remain planning evidence only; no candidate, provisional, accepted, or stable status may be assigned.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Exact candidate binding proves identity only and does not prove semantic correctness, approval, implementation, conformance, deployment, or institutional adoption.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only when a declared private dependency is material; public identity binding does not disclose private doctrine

requirements.title: Explicit normative sections
requirements.normative_statement:
An AAOS RFC MUST identify which sections and requirement records are normative and which content is informative.

Examples, rationale, diagrams, notes, and historical descriptions MUST NOT silently create normative obligations.
requirements.positive_case: Every normative section and record is explicitly marked, and informative material is separately marked.
requirements.negative_case: An example, rationale, diagram, note, or historical passage is treated as an unstated normative obligation.
requirements.adversarial_case: Uppercase keywords are inserted into an informative note and presented as independently binding.
requirements.unknown_case: A section lacks an explicit normative or informative classification.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Treat the unclassified content as non-authorizing and block conformance evaluation that depends on it.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Ambiguous normative scope blocks exact semantic review.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Section classification does not itself prove compliance with any normative clause.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; private material remains outside the public normative scope unless represented by a bounded public-safe dependency

requirements.title: Normative keyword profile
requirements.normative_statement:
AAOS RFCs MUST interpret uppercase normative keywords according to RFC 2119 as clarified by RFC 8174, subject to the AAOS requirement-record rules.

AAOS-authored normative text SHOULD use:

MUST
MUST NOT
SHOULD
SHOULD NOT
MAY

New AAOS normative text SHOULD NOT use `SHALL` or `SHALL NOT`.
required_evidence: human semantic review of keyword use
requirements.positive_case: Uppercase normative keywords in explicitly normative content use the canonical AAOS profile.
requirements.negative_case: Lowercase or informative prose is treated as normative without explicit incorporation.
requirements.adversarial_case: New text uses SHALL to evade canonical keyword validation or presents MAY as authorization.
requirements.unknown_case: Keyword context cannot be classified as normative or informative.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block semantic interpretation until context and keyword force are explicitly resolved.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Ambiguous or noncanonical normative force requires correction before disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Normative keyword use defines specification force only; it does not grant operational or institutional Authority.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: SHOULD-exception record
requirements.normative_statement: An implementation or profile that does not follow a `SHOULD` or `SHOULD NOT` requirement MUST record the justification, affected requirement IDs, risk, compensating behavior, evidence, scope, and validity interval.
required_inputs: affected SHOULD or SHOULD NOT requirement
requirements.positive_case: Every deviation from SHOULD or SHOULD NOT has a complete bounded exception record.
requirements.negative_case: A deviation is undocumented or omits affected requirements, evidence, scope, or validity.
requirements.adversarial_case: A SHOULD-exception record is presented as a waiver, risk acceptance, or Authority expansion.
requirements.unknown_case: The exception's scope, validity, evidence, or approving abstract Authority reference cannot be evaluated.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not recognize the exception; apply the more restrictive requirement interpretation pending review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Undocumented or incomplete exception evidence cannot support the claimed deviation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: SHOULD exception documented != waiver != risk acceptance != authority expansion
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; any institution-specific decision remains a private bounded dependency and is not inferred from the public record

requirements.title: One primary requirement identifier
requirements.normative_statement: Every normative obligation MUST have exactly one primary `AAOS-REQ-<RFC>-<NUMBER>` identifier.
requirements.positive_case: Each normative obligation resolves to exactly one primary AAOS requirement ID.
requirements.negative_case: A normative obligation has no primary ID or more than one competing primary ID.
requirements.adversarial_case: A specialized contract or test ID is substituted for the primary requirement ID.
requirements.unknown_case: A normative obligation cannot be matched deterministically to a registry record.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block package consistency and require explicit mapping.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Missing or duplicate primary identity blocks requirement traceability.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A primary ID establishes traceability, not implementation, verification, conformance, or Authority.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: Specialized identifier mapping
requirements.normative_statement:
Specialized contract, interface, conformance, fixture, test, schema, evidence-class, and record identifiers MUST map to a primary AAOS requirement where they implement or evaluate a normative obligation.

Examples include:

TG-IFACE-*
TG-CONF-*
TG-CONTRACT-EV-*
TG-FIX-*
TG-AP-*
TG-AR-*
TG-TP-*

They MUST NOT become competing primary IDs for the same obligation.
requirements.positive_case: Every specialized identifier that implements or evaluates an obligation maps to its primary AAOS requirement.
requirements.negative_case: A specialized identifier has no primary mapping or is represented as a competing primary obligation.
requirements.adversarial_case: Renaming a specialized identifier is used to create a second primary requirement for duplicate prose.
requirements.unknown_case: The relationship between a specialized record and normative obligation cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Treat the specialized artifact as untraced and prohibit requirement or conformance claims based on it.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unmapped or competing identifiers fail traceability validation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A mapping shows declared relation only and does not prove that the specialized artifact correctly implements or evaluates the requirement.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; private identifiers may be abstracted but are not fabricated or disclosed

requirements.title: Requirement identifier immutability
requirements.normative_statement:
A requirement identifier MUST NOT be reused for unrelated or materially incompatible semantics.

Editorial and non-semantic clarification MAY preserve the ID. A breaking semantic replacement MUST create an explicit successor requirement or major RFC revision.
required_evidence: predecessor and successor mapping for replacement
requirements.positive_case: Editorial or non-semantic clarification preserves the ID, while breaking replacement creates a successor or major revision.
requirements.negative_case: An existing ID is reused for unrelated or materially incompatible semantics.
requirements.adversarial_case: A breaking change is labeled editorial or clarifying to retain an incompatible ID.
requirements.unknown_case: Semantic compatibility with the predecessor cannot be determined.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not reuse the ID; require explicit semantic review and a restrictive successor path.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incompatible ID reuse blocks the registry as a contract contradiction.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Identifier continuity does not prove semantic compatibility without the required review evidence.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: Historical resolvability
requirements.normative_statement: Deprecated, superseded, withdrawn, and rejected requirement identifiers MUST remain historically resolvable with their prior text, version, status, and successor references.
requirements.positive_case: Every inactive requirement ID resolves to its preserved historical record and successor relation.
requirements.negative_case: A deprecated, superseded, withdrawn, or rejected ID is deleted or loses its prior text or status.
requirements.adversarial_case: A new requirement replaces an old record in place and erases incompatible historical semantics.
requirements.unknown_case: Historical text, version, status, or successor evidence is incomplete.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Return not_evaluable for history-dependent claims and prohibit silent substitution.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Missing historical resolution blocks migration and predecessor claims.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Historical resolvability preserves evidence; it does not keep an inactive requirement currently applicable.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: Requirement verification classification
requirements.normative_statement:
Every normative requirement MUST declare one or more verification classes:

machine_testable
evidence_reviewable
human_disposition_only
private_l0_dependent

It MUST also declare required evidence, negative behavior, unknown behavior, and public claim boundary.
required_evidence: at least one allowed verification class
requirements.positive_case: A requirement record declares allowed verification classes and all mandatory evaluation and claim-boundary fields.
requirements.negative_case: A normative requirement omits its verification class, evidence, negative behavior, unknown behavior, or public claim boundary.
requirements.adversarial_case: An untestable requirement is labeled machine_testable to bypass required human or private review.
requirements.unknown_case: The correct verification class or evidence minimum cannot be determined.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Classify the record as incomplete and block its use for conformance or lifecycle evidence.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete verification metadata fails registry structure validation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A declared verification class identifies a method; it does not establish that verification passed or authorize any lifecycle or operational action.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional when and only when private_l0_dependent is explicitly declared

requirements.title: Namespace separation
requirements.normative_statement: `rfc_class`, `rfc_review_state`, `rfc_status`, archival designation, delivery state, implementation state, conformance outcome, and deployment state MUST remain separate namespaces.
required_inputs: delivery, implementation, conformance, and deployment state definitions
requirements.positive_case: Every class, review, lifecycle, archival, delivery, implementation, conformance, and deployment value is represented in its own namespace.
requirements.negative_case: An experimental class is treated as lifecycle status, a blocked review as rejection, or issue closure as acceptance.
requirements.adversarial_case: A passing deployment or conformance state is copied into rfc_status.
requirements.unknown_case: A state value has no declared namespace or has conflicting definitions.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject the collapsed state representation and require explicit namespace resolution.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Namespace collapse blocks lifecycle interpretation and disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A value in one namespace proves nothing about any other namespace.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; private applicability remains a separate bounded relation

requirements.title: RFC class registry
requirements.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define the following initial RFC classes or explicitly disposition each alternative:

process
standards_track
profile
informational
experimental

RFC class MUST NOT imply lifecycle status, implementation maturity, deployment suitability, or conformance.
required_inputs: human dispositions for any proposed alternative
required_evidence: presence of all five initial classes or an explicit disposition for each alternative
requirements.positive_case: The registry defines process, standards_track, profile, informational, and experimental without implying any other state.
requirements.negative_case: A required class is missing without disposition or is used as a lifecycle, maturity, deployment, or conformance claim.
requirements.adversarial_case: Experimental is presented as a transition between accepted and deprecated.
requirements.unknown_case: An undeclared or extension class lacks a reviewed namespace definition.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Treat the class as unsupported and require explicit human disposition; do not map it to a known permissive class.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete or conflated class semantics block class-dependent review rules.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: RFC class describes specification category only.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: RFC review-state registry
requirements.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define an RFC review-state namespace that includes at least:

not_started
under_review
changes_requested
ready_for_disposition
blocked_pending_private_l0_input
blocked_by_contract_contradiction

Review state MUST NOT create normative force.
required_evidence: presence and definitions of the minimum review states
requirements.positive_case: All minimum review states are defined and carry review readiness only.
requirements.negative_case: A review state is omitted or represented as normative force or lifecycle disposition.
requirements.adversarial_case: ready_for_disposition is presented as accepted, or blocked_pending_private_l0_input as rejected.
requirements.unknown_case: A review record supplies an unknown state value.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject the unknown value and retain the last valid review state without lifecycle change.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Invalid review-state semantics block review progression.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Review state describes process readiness and never assigns RFC status.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only for the abstract blocked_pending_private_l0_input state; no private identity or doctrine is disclosed

requirements.title: RFC lifecycle-status registry
requirements.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define an RFC lifecycle-status namespace that includes at least:

draft
candidate
provisional
accepted
stable
deprecated
superseded
withdrawn
rejected

Each status MUST have a definition, permitted predecessor and successor states, transition authority, required evidence, and migration consequence.
requirements.positive_case: Every lifecycle status has complete transition, authority, evidence, and migration metadata.
requirements.negative_case: A lifecycle status is missing a definition or uses an unresolved eligible wildcard for its predecessors.
requirements.adversarial_case: A status is entered from an unlisted state or without the class-specific evidence and human disposition.
requirements.unknown_case: A status, source state, authority reference, or evidence rule is unknown.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Prohibit transition and preserve the last valid status.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete lifecycle semantics block any affected transition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Lifecycle status has specification meaning only and does not authorize implementation, conformance, release, deployment, or institutional adoption.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional where a transition explicitly depends on private institutional applicability; exact private assignments remain undisclosed

requirements.title: Explicit status transitions
requirements.normative_statement:
No RFC lifecycle transition MAY be inferred from GitHub state, publication, CI, evaluator output, runtime behavior, implementation availability, conformance test results, or elapsed time.

Every material transition MUST use an exact-candidate-bound human disposition record.
requirements.positive_case: An enumerated transition occurs only after an exact-candidate-bound human disposition.
requirements.negative_case: A merge, publication, passing check, runtime observation, implementation, test result, or elapsed time is treated as the transition.
requirements.adversarial_case: A draft PR is merged or CI passes and automation assigns accepted or stable status.
requirements.unknown_case: The human disposer, exact candidate, or applicable evidence cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: No transition occurs; retain the prior lifecycle status or not_assigned state.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Inferred transition is invalid and must not be recorded.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Review, repository, CI, runtime, and test evidence cannot independently assign RFC lifecycle status.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only when the transition rule declares bounded private review; no public evidence substitutes for it

requirements.title: Historical projection
requirements.normative_statement: `historical` MUST be represented as a derived archival designation or publication projection, not as a competing RFC class and lifecycle status.
required_evidence: absence of historical from class and lifecycle registries
requirements.positive_case: Historical is derived from the applicable inactive record and remains separate from class and status.
requirements.negative_case: Historical is encoded as an RFC class or lifecycle status.
requirements.adversarial_case: A historical publication label is used to erase or replace the underlying withdrawn or superseded status.
requirements.unknown_case: The underlying archival source state cannot be resolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not derive the historical projection until the source record is resolved.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Invalid projection fails namespace validation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Historical is a view and does not alter prior status, force, or evidence.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none

requirements.title: Review-role separation
requirements.normative_statement: RFC-0000 MUST distinguish proposer, editor, semantic owner, required reviewers, private-L0 reviewer where applicable, and final disposer.
requirements.positive_case: Each abstract review role is distinct in the contract and any permitted combination records its limitation.
requirements.negative_case: Proposer, editor, reviewer, and final disposer are treated as interchangeable by default.
requirements.adversarial_case: An editor claims final-disposition Authority solely from authorship.
requirements.unknown_case: Required abstract role or separation evidence is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block final disposition while preserving the candidate for further review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unresolved or invalid separation of duties prevents lifecycle disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: The public contract defines abstract roles only and does not identify private institutional role holders.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; exact private role assignments remain private and must not be inferred

requirements.title: No self-acceptance by default
requirements.normative_statement:
A proposer or editor MUST NOT assign `accepted` or `stable` status solely by virtue of proposing or editing the RFC.

Any permitted role combination MUST be explicitly authorized and its independence limitation MUST be recorded.
required_evidence: independent final disposition or explicit combination authorization
requirements.positive_case: Acceptance or stability is assigned by the authorized final disposer with required independence evidence.
requirements.negative_case: A proposer or editor self-assigns accepted or stable solely from authorship.
requirements.adversarial_case: A role is renamed or combined without disclosure to conceal self-acceptance.
requirements.unknown_case: Role identity, authorization, or independence limitation is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Prohibit accepted or stable disposition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Invalid self-acceptance has no lifecycle effect.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Authorship and editing do not confer lifecycle disposition Authority.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; private authorization may be represented only by a bounded abstract reference without role identity disclosure

requirements.title: Disposition record contract
required_evidence: repository identity or explicit not_materialized state
requirements.positive_case: The disposition record contains every mandatory identity, evidence, Authority, time, constraint, unresolved-input, and consequence field.
requirements.negative_case: A disposition omits candidate or requirement identity, review evidence, Authority reference, time context, or lifecycle consequences.
requirements.adversarial_case: A mutable issue or PR state is substituted for an exact digest-bound disposition.
requirements.unknown_case: Any mandatory disposition input is unresolved or absent.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject the disposition record as incomplete and perform no lifecycle transition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete disposition evidence cannot assign RFC status.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A complete record documents a human disposition; it does not authorize implementation, conformance, release, deployment, risk acceptance, restore, cutover, or sealing.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; the final-disposer and private-review references remain abstract and reveal no private doctrine

requirements.title: Planning review versus RFC status
requirements.normative_statement: A review of mutable issue text MAY assign only planning review state. It MUST NOT assign `candidate`, `provisional`, `accepted`, or `stable` RFC status until an immutable candidate identity exists.
required_evidence: proof that any lifecycle disposition subject is immutable and exactly bound
requirements.positive_case: Mutable issue review records only planning state while rfc_status remains not_assigned.
requirements.negative_case: Mutable issue text is assigned candidate, provisional, accepted, or stable status.
requirements.adversarial_case: A branch name, issue title, or document presence is described as immutable candidate identity.
requirements.unknown_case: Candidate immutability or digest binding cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Limit the result to planning review state and assign no RFC lifecycle status.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Premature lifecycle disposition is invalid.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Planning review state is not RFC lifecycle status and does not establish publication, acceptance, implementation, conformance, or deployment.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none for identity; any private review remains a separate bounded dependency

requirements.title: Independent revision classification
requirements.normative_statement:
Every RFC revision MUST declare both a semantic `revision_class` and a resulting RFC version.

Initial revision classes MUST include:

editorial
clarifying
additive_compatible
restrictive
breaking
requirements.positive_case: A revision separately declares an allowed semantic class and the resulting RFC version increment.
requirements.negative_case: The semantic change class is absent, or a version number is used to define the change semantics.
requirements.adversarial_case: A breaking semantic change is hidden behind a PATCH or MINOR label.
requirements.unknown_case: The semantic impact cannot be determined from the supplied change evidence.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not classify the revision as compatible; block disposition pending semantic review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Conflated or unknown change classification blocks version assignment.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A version increment records the result of review and does not independently define semantic compatibility.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only when the revision affects a declared private dependency; no private threshold is inferred

requirements.title: Semantic versioning baseline
requirements.normative_statement:
AAOS RFC versions SHOULD use `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`.

Default mapping:

editorial or non-conformance-impact clarification
→ PATCH

additive compatible
→ MINOR

restrictive change that invalidates prior conformant behavior
→ MAJOR unless an accepted transition profile preserves bounded compatibility

breaking
→ MAJOR or successor RFC
required_evidence: bounded compatibility profile evidence for any restrictive exception
requirements.positive_case: The resulting version follows MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH and the reviewed semantic class's default mapping.
requirements.negative_case: A restrictive or breaking change is assigned a lower increment without an accepted bounded mechanism.
requirements.adversarial_case: A MINOR number is presented as proof of compatibility without semantic review.
requirements.unknown_case: Change impact or compatibility-preservation evidence is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Use no permissive mapping; require explicit human semantic disposition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unsupported version mapping blocks revision disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Semantic version syntax does not itself prove compatibility, conformance, or implementation.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only where institutional applicability is affected; no public version implies private adoption

requirements.title: Clarification cannot hide semantic change
requirements.normative_statement: A revision MUST NOT be classified as `clarifying` when it changes required evidence, permitted Authority, object or state meaning, failure behavior, interface obligations, conformance outcomes, or public claim boundaries.
required_inputs: prior and proposed evidence, Authority, object, state, failure, interface, conformance, and claim-boundary definitions
requirements.positive_case: A clarification changes no listed semantic or conformance dimension.
requirements.negative_case: A change to any listed dimension is labeled clarifying.
requirements.adversarial_case: Small textual edits change fail-closed behavior or public claim boundaries while retaining a PATCH label.
requirements.unknown_case: Any listed impact cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject clarifying classification and require a more restrictive change-class review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Misclassification blocks the proposed version increment and disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Textual size and labels do not prove absence of semantic change.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional if permitted Authority or private applicability is affected; exact doctrine remains undisclosed

requirements.title: Emergency Constraint Notice
requirements.positive_case: A bounded, time-limited ECN contains every mandatory identity, restriction, Authority, evidence, impact, and follow-up field.
requirements.negative_case: An urgent restriction silently edits accepted RFC text or omits mandatory ECN fields.
requirements.adversarial_case: An ECN is used as an undocumented permanent semantic revision.
requirements.unknown_case: Threat, affected scope, Authority, validity, evidence, or follow-up cannot be evaluated.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not infer expanded permission; block ECN effectiveness until mandatory inputs are resolved, while existing independent safety restrictions remain unaffected.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete ECN cannot serve as the RFC emergency restriction record.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: An ECN records a temporary restriction and does not silently revise the RFC or prove runtime enforcement.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; issuing Authority may be an abstract private reference and no institutional role identity is assigned

requirements.title: ECN cannot widen authority
requirements.normative_statement: An Emergency Constraint Notice MUST NOT expand Authority, waive mandatory evidence, convert `not_evaluable` into a permissive result, or become permanent without a normal RFC revision and disposition.
requirements.positive_case: The ECN only restricts or suspends within its bounded validity and preserves mandatory evidence and unknown behavior.
requirements.negative_case: The ECN expands Authority, waives evidence, maps not_evaluable to allow, or persists without normal revision.
requirements.adversarial_case: Repeated renewals or a missing expiry are used to make an ECN permanent.
requirements.unknown_case: Authority scope, evidence effect, unknown handling, or validity cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Apply no permissive effect and require bounded human review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: The ECN is invalid for the prohibited effect; existing independent restrictions are not automatically removed.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: ECN existence does not prove runtime restriction, institutional adoption, deployment control, or risk acceptance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional; private applicability may further restrict but never expands the public ECN contract

requirements.title: Public semantic precedence
requirements.normative_statement:
RFC-0000 MUST define public semantic precedence so that lower-level artifacts cannot redefine an accepted public Semantic Core requirement.

Initial precedence candidate:

accepted RFC
→ accepted RFC-owned requirement registry
→ accepted ADR interpretation where delegated by the RFC
→ implementation contract
→ schema, API, or event representation
→ tests, fixtures, and validators
→ runtime behavior
→ informational documentation
→ historical milestone text
requirements.positive_case: Every lower-level public artifact traces upward and preserves the accepted RFC-owned semantics.
requirements.negative_case: A schema, test, runtime behavior, document, or milestone silently redefines a public Semantic Core requirement.
requirements.adversarial_case: Observed runtime behavior is treated as the canonical semantic source over the accepted RFC.
requirements.unknown_case: Artifact ownership, delegation, or precedence cannot be resolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block the lower-level semantic claim and require explicit contradiction disposition.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Lower-level redefinition is invalid and may produce blocked_by_contract_contradiction.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Precedence defines semantic ownership and does not prove implementation, test correctness, conformance, deployment, or adoption.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none for public semantic precedence; institutional applicability is separately governed

requirements.title: Private institutional applicability
requirements.normative_statement:
Private L0 doctrine MAY determine whether, where, under whose Authority, and with which restrictions an institution adopts a public RFC or profile.

It MUST NOT silently rewrite the public RFC's canonical semantics.
required_inputs: bounded private review receipt when applicable
requirements.positive_case: A private applicability decision restricts or declines adoption while leaving canonical public semantics unchanged.
requirements.negative_case: Private doctrine silently rewrites the public RFC's canonical semantics.
requirements.adversarial_case: A public document invents a private L0 role, trusted root, risk threshold, or institutional Authority.
requirements.unknown_case: Required private applicability input is unavailable or cannot be safely represented.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Use blocked_pending_private_l0_input, non-adoption, or a bounded restriction; do not infer permission.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: No institutional adoption claim may be made without the required bounded private review.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: The public receipt proves only the declared bounded review result and does not disclose or prove full private implementation.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: required only for the declared institutional applicability question; private role assignments, trusted roots, thresholds, topology, and procedures remain undisclosed

requirements.title: Public/private contradiction behavior
requirements.normative_statement:
A material contradiction between a public RFC and required private doctrine MUST result in bounded profile restriction, non-adoption, `blocked_pending_private_l0_input`, or `blocked_by_contract_contradiction`.

It MUST NOT use last-write-wins, newest-document-wins, or silent semantic override.
required_evidence: explicit restriction, non-adoption, or blocking disposition
requirements.positive_case: The contradiction produces one of the enumerated restrictive or blocking outcomes.
requirements.negative_case: The newest, last-written, or private document silently overwrites canonical public semantics.
requirements.adversarial_case: A fabricated private rule is invoked to bypass a public mandatory requirement.
requirements.unknown_case: The private input needed to establish or resolve the contradiction is unavailable.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Use blocked_pending_private_l0_input or a more restrictive bounded outcome.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Contradictory adoption or claim is blocked until explicit human disposition.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: The public record may state only a bounded contradiction result and must not disclose or reconstruct private doctrine.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: required for the declared contradiction review only; no private semantics are inferred

requirements.title: No public overclaim
requirements.normative_statement: Acceptance of a public RFC MUST NOT be represented as disclosure, implementation evidence, or independent verification of private sovereign controls.
required_inputs: bounded private review receipt when referenced
required_evidence: absence of private implementation or verification overclaim
requirements.positive_case: Public acceptance claims remain limited to the public RFC lifecycle disposition.
requirements.negative_case: Public RFC acceptance is described as proof or disclosure of private controls.
requirements.adversarial_case: A bounded private review receipt is expanded into a claim of full private implementation.
requirements.unknown_case: A public statement's private-control implication cannot be bounded.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject or restrict the public claim pending explicit boundary review.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Overclaiming language must not be published as valid evidence.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Public RFC acceptance != private disclosure != private implementation evidence != independent private-control verification.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: private evidence remains outside the public claim except for a sanitized bounded result

requirements.title: Artifact-type separation
requirements.normative_statement: Issue, ADR, RFC, requirement registry, schema, interface contract, test, fixture, validator, runtime control, conformance claim, deployment admission, and institutional decision MUST remain distinct artifact types.
requirements.positive_case: Each listed artifact has its own identity, type, lifecycle, and traceability relations.
requirements.negative_case: One artifact's existence or result is treated as another artifact type or disposition.
requirements.adversarial_case: A passing test is presented as a conformance claim or an issue is presented as the RFC itself.
requirements.unknown_case: An artifact's type or ownership cannot be resolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not infer a type; block claims that depend on the unresolved classification.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Collapsed artifact identities fail package traceability.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Artifact relations do not collapse artifact meanings, evidence, or Authority.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; institutional decisions may be referenced only abstractly

requirements.title: Downstream traceability
requirements.normative_statement: Every schema, interface contract, event contract, validator, test, fixture, runtime control, and conformance claim that implements or evaluates an RFC obligation MUST trace to stable RFC and primary requirement IDs.
requirements.positive_case: Each downstream implementing or evaluating artifact resolves to the stable RFC and applicable primary requirements.
requirements.negative_case: A downstream artifact lacks either RFC or primary requirement traceability.
requirements.adversarial_case: A mutable path, title, or specialized ID is used instead of the stable RFC and primary requirement IDs.
requirements.unknown_case: The obligation implemented or evaluated by an artifact cannot be identified.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Treat the artifact as untraced and prohibit implementation or conformance claims derived from it.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Missing downstream traceability fails package and claim validation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: A traceability edge declares relation only; it does not prove semantic correctness, runtime enforcement, or conformance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none for public traceability; private artifacts may use bounded abstract references without disclosure

requirements.title: Test result boundary
requirements.normative_statement: Passing a schema validator, evaluator, fixture, test suite, or CI workflow MUST NOT by itself establish live control enforcement, institutional adoption, deployment authorization, certification, or risk acceptance.
required_evidence: separate evidence and Authority for any live, adoption, deployment, certification, or risk claim
requirements.positive_case: A passing result is reported only for its exact evaluated subject, environment, and method.
requirements.negative_case: A passing result alone is used to claim live enforcement, adoption, deployment authorization, certification, or risk acceptance.
requirements.adversarial_case: CI success on fixtures is represented as production-control operation.
requirements.unknown_case: Evaluated subject, environment, method, or claim scope is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Reject the broader claim and retain only the bounded test result if its own identity is established.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unsupported live or institutional claim must be removed or blocked.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: test pass != live control enforcement != institutional adoption != deployment authorization != certification != risk acceptance
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; institutional claims require separate bounded Authority evidence

requirements.title: RFC status boundary
requirements.normative_statement: `accepted` or `stable` RFC status MUST NOT authorize implementation, release, deployment, certification, risk acceptance, waiver, audit closure, rollback, restore, cutover, or proof sealing.
requirements.positive_case: RFC status is reported only as specification lifecycle state.
requirements.negative_case: Accepted or stable status is used as authorization for any listed action.
requirements.adversarial_case: A deployment, restore, or sealing action cites the RFC disposition as its sole approval.
requirements.unknown_case: Separate action Authority or evidence cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not authorize or imply the action.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: The unsupported authorization or claim is invalid and must be blocked.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: RFC lifecycle disposition is non-authorizing for implementation, release, deployment, certification, risk, waiver, audit, rollback, restore, cutover, and sealing.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; any institutional action requires separate private Authority outside this public RFC

requirements.title: Unknown mandatory semantics
requirements.normative_statement:
An unsupported or unknown mandatory RFC semantic MUST result in `fail_closed`, `not_evaluable`, or explicit human disposition according to the applicable profile.

It MUST NOT be ignored, guessed, downgraded, or mapped to the nearest permissive behavior.
requirements.positive_case: Unknown mandatory semantics produce only an allowed restrictive result.
requirements.negative_case: An unknown mandatory value is ignored, guessed, downgraded, or mapped permissively.
requirements.adversarial_case: A near-match or unsupported extension is intentionally supplied to trigger an allow path.
requirements.unknown_case: The mandatory semantic is unsupported or unrecognized.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Return fail_closed, not_evaluable, or require explicit human disposition according to the profile.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: No permissive behavior, transition, conformance, or action may be inferred.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Restrictive handling of unknown semantics does not itself prove implementation correctness or authorize action.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional where an applicable profile is privately governed; no private default is inferred

requirements.title: Semantic Core intake restriction
requirements.normative_statement:
A new Semantic Core RFC or primary issue MUST be justified by an unowned irreducible concept, canonical contradiction, necessary breaking change, authority-boundary or fail-closed defect, or independently reviewable profile after base stabilization.

External novelty alone MUST NOT create a normative RFC.
required_evidence: one or more permitted intake grounds
base-stabilization evidence for an independent profile
requirements.positive_case: A new Semantic Core proposal identifies a permitted, independently reviewable justification.
requirements.negative_case: A proposal relies only on novelty, popularity, implementation availability, or external terminology.
requirements.adversarial_case: A duplicate concept is renamed to bypass ownership and contradiction analysis.
requirements.unknown_case: Ownership, irreducibility, contradiction, breaking necessity, defect, or profile independence cannot be established.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not admit the proposal as a new Semantic Core RFC or primary issue.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unsupported intake remains non-normative planning material.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Intake eligibility permits review only and does not assign RFC status or authorize implementation.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only if an authority-boundary defect depends on private input; represent it as a sanitized bounded constraint

requirements.title: Append-only specification history
requirements.normative_statement: Accepted RFC versions, requirement records, dispositions, and conformance claims MUST remain historically preserved after deprecation, supersession, withdrawal, or rejection.
requirements.positive_case: Inactive versions, requirements, dispositions, and claims remain resolvable without mutation.
requirements.negative_case: Deprecation, supersession, withdrawal, or rejection deletes or overwrites historical evidence.
requirements.adversarial_case: History is rewritten so a prior incompatible claim appears to target the successor.
requirements.unknown_case: A historical object or its exact identity cannot be located.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block history-dependent migration and successor claims.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Loss or mutation of required history fails preservation and traceability validation.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Historical preservation does not imply current validity or successor conformance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; private evidence locations may remain opaque while bounded references are preserved

requirements.title: Migration-effect declaration
requirements.normative_statement: Deprecation, supersession, restrictive revision, breaking revision, ECN issuance, and requirement withdrawal MUST declare effects on implementations, profiles, schemas, tests, deployment admissions, conformance claims, and public claim language.
required_evidence: declared effect for every listed subject
requirements.positive_case: Every applicable lifecycle or change event explicitly declares all implementation, profile, representation, test, admission, conformance, and public-claim effects.
requirements.negative_case: An event omits one or more required impact categories.
requirements.adversarial_case: A successor version is published without disclosing invalidated claims or required migration.
requirements.unknown_case: An affected subject, dependency, or claim effect cannot be determined.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Block the affected transition or change disposition pending complete migration analysis.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Incomplete effect declaration prevents valid migration or successor claims.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Declared migration effects do not themselves authorize deployment, rollback, restore, cutover, or risk acceptance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional where institutional adoption or deployment admissions are affected; no private procedure is specified

requirements.title: No successor-conformance inference
requirements.normative_statement: A conformance claim against one RFC version MUST NOT be represented as conformance to a successor RFC unless the successor requirement set has been evaluated against the exact implementation and environment.
required_evidence: successor evaluation against the exact subject and environment
requirements.positive_case: A successor conformance claim is made only after exact-subject evaluation against the successor set.
requirements.negative_case: A prior-version claim is relabeled as successor conformance without reevaluation.
requirements.adversarial_case: The same product name is used to conceal a changed implementation or environment.
requirements.unknown_case: Successor requirement identity, implementation identity, environment, or evaluation evidence is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not make or preserve a successor conformance claim.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: Unsupported successor claim is invalid.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Prior conformance and same product name do not establish successor conformance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: none; any institutional admission remains separate

requirements.title: Bounded migration windows
requirements.normative_statement:
A migration window MAY preserve the validity of a historical claim only within its original RFC version, evaluated subject, environment, restrictions, and validity interval.

It MUST NOT imply conformance to the successor RFC.
requirements.positive_case: The historical claim remains bounded to its original exact version, subject, environment, restrictions, and time.
requirements.negative_case: A migration window broadens the historical claim or implies successor conformance.
requirements.adversarial_case: An open-ended migration window is used to avoid successor evaluation.
requirements.unknown_case: Any original claim boundary or validity input is unresolved.
requirements.not_evaluable_behavior: Do not preserve or broaden the historical claim.
requirements.failure_or_restrictive_consequence: The migration claim is invalid outside its exact original bounds.
requirements.public_claim_boundary: Historical validity within a migration window is not successor RFC conformance.
requirements.private_l0_dependency: conditional only for separate institutional applicability; the public migration boundary does not infer private adoption
